text: Good evening. Let's take a moment to reflect on your day.
How would you describe your day overall?
When things went well today, what made the biggest difference?
When things didn’t go as planned, where did your attention go?
options: What others did or didn’t do

text: You recognized your role in shaping outcomes today. That sense of agency is a powerful driver of growth.
It felt like events were outside your control. Even so, there may have been small choices within your reach worth noticing.
You noticed both your effort and external support. Seeing both sides gives a balanced perspective.
Now let’s shift from how things happened to what you contributed.
Which moment best reflects how you showed up today?
options: I helped someone without being asked
I focused on completing my tasks
I felt I needed more support from others

text: How did you approach going beyond your responsibilities?
options: I took initiative to contribute more
I did what was expected of me

text: You contributed beyond your immediate role. These actions build trust and strengthen teams.
You stayed within your responsibilities. There may be opportunities to contribute a little more next time.
Your focus leaned toward what you needed from others. Reflecting on what you can give may shift that balance.
Now let’s expand the lens and look beyond yourself.
Who was most impacted by your work today?
When you faced challenges, who did you keep in mind?
Your focus stayed mostly on yourself today. Expanding your perspective can bring more meaning to your work.
You considered your team in your thinking. That shared awareness strengthens collaboration.
You focused beyond yourself toward others. This broader perspective is a strong source of purpose.
Today you reflected on your sense of control, your contribution, and your impact on others. Small shifts in awareness here can meaningfully shape how you show up tomorrow.
See you tomorrow.
